stem: What is the difference between a RIB and a FIB?
choice_a: A. The FIB is populated based on RIB content.
choice_b: B. The RIB maintains a mirror image of the FIB.
choice_c: C. The RIB is used to make IP source prefix-based switching decisions.
choice_d: D. The FIB is where all IP routing information is stored.
topic_notes: Tier C research — community recall; verify answer on Cisco Tier A only

stem: Which protocol is used as the control plane for a Cisco SD-Access fabric?
topic_notes: Tier B — verify answer on Cisco Tier A
